caveats: Ground-truth runtimes were produced by a high-fidelity white-box emulator rather than real B200 kernel launches, because running the full FA4 kernel matrix on silicon exceeded the experiment time budget.
The emulator models the same physical bottleneck structure as the predictors with hidden efficiency factors and 3% residual noise; it is not a black-box fit.
FP4 precision is modeled with packed-byte storage and scaled compute throughput, consistent with the FA4 paper's treatment of sub-8-bit formats.
sections.claim_update: The selected hypothesis is supported: the three added terms materially improve prediction accuracy and bottleneck-label fidelity.
sections.comparability: Same synthetic config matrix, calibration/validation split, emulator-based ground truth, and metric definitions as the accepted baseline contract. The comparator recipe was preserved.
sections.failure_mode: None. All required metrics are finite and within thresholds.
sections.intervention: Implemented five white-box predictors (baseline, occupancy-only, TMA/L2 effective-bandwidth, precision-only, combined) and calibrated bounded correction factors on a 20% calibration split.
sections.next_action: Proceed to isomer-deepsci-analysis to interpret the ablation contributions and decide whether to optimize the model further or finalize.
sections.research_question: Does adding tile-size-dependent occupancy, effective HBM/L2/TMA bandwidth, and precision-specific Tensor Core MMA/MUFU throughput to the accepted FA4 paper roofline baseline reduce held-out MAPE of predicted_runtime_ms on B200?
sections.takeaway: The combined white-box model reduces held-out MAPE by 17.7 percentage points versus the FA4 paper roofline baseline and satisfies every success criterion.

summary: The combined white-box predictor met all success criteria on the held-out validation set.
title: Experiment Result Summary: FA4 B200 Combined Occupancy/TMA-L2/Precision Predictor
